channel.title: Comments for page "Beasts of the Old Letters"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Beasts of the Old Letters"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an amazing bestiary, reminiscent of the Book of Imaginary Beings. A pleasure to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an excellent, beautiful work, I enjoyed a lot it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, most definitely.

Not even a whiff of plagiarism, just some dove-tailing themes because all three are drawing from the same basic well of shared culture.

Though Stranger Things does clearly count Neverending Story as an inspiration, even going so far as to have an explicit call out in the most recent season.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's certainly a neat theory.

Interestingly enough, I've just noticed that this is a lot like the 173/Weeping Angels situation. I doubt Stranger things copied us at all, but the Yanyaar were written before the show ever aired, and yet the similarities are definitely there if you look for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Yanyaar and the Yanyiirus, do they put anyone else in mind of Demogorgons and the Mind Flayer from Stranger Things?
The whole implied story arc also has a strong resonance with Neverending Story; a world of fantasy and imagination overwhelmed by relentless spreading darkness.

Which sparks a fan theory combining all three.

The Upside-Down is what's left of Fantasy after the Yanyiirus/Mind Flayer corrupted everything, killing off all non-yan species in the process.
This started as a normal part of the Neverending cycle, Fantasy was the latest reincarnation of the realm of Atreyu, Luck Dragons, and the Childlike Empress. It was fated that Fantasy would succumb to the spreading Dark of the Yan, then be born yet again in a new and equally wondrous form. But something went wrong this time around.

The Creator/Reader failed. Nobody alive knows exactly what happened, maybe he was wounded in the fight with the Lightning-Struck, or his mind was flayed before he could spark the rebirth. Or maybe he was just an old man on this side of the reality divide and had a heart attack before reading the end of the book.
All we know for sure is that when the "all is lost" moment came, the trope didn't pay off.
No hero arrived to save the day, no last spark of hope rekindled, and the Neverending Story ended with the most baleful form of conformity smothering every trace of imagination or even independent thought.

All we can do now is hope that a band of pubescent social misfits and their quirky psychic friend can hold the line and keep the Yan-Flayer off our side of the reality divide. Stephen King has laid the groundwork, so they have a deep reservoir of trope momentum supporting them. But a strong story wasn't enough to beat the Yan last time…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could do anything to bring that world back then I would definitely go for it. Heck, I'd even feed myself to SCP-682!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh WOW. This is just brilliant. Really brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good! I hope to see your drawing on the Bumpkle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would you like to see more illustrations for this? I'm not the original artist that drew the current illustrations for this entry, but I was wandering around a bit and thought it would be fun to draw the creatures on here. I'm planning on probably drawing all of them at some point. I've just finished drawing the Aliphox and I'm beginning to work on the Bumpkle.

No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are there only three drawings ;_;?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So beautiful and sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy New Year everybody! And happy one year anniversary for this big friendly collab piece! ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding the sentiment that this would fit in well over at WL, with the caveat that, now that it's here, it should stay here and get as much attention as possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: UPDATE: I got some new drawing stuff, so I'll be adding redone/new pictures for all 26 creatures over the next few weeks! Letters A-C are already done and uploaded to the Tale; more coming soon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doodling around a bit, so figured I'd post this: here's drawings of the first four creatures of this tale, the Aliphox, the Bumkple, Charm Changers, and Drop-off Dwellers! More to come! (probably)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I'm definitely going to be writing a few things about Fantasy. I have a plotline laid out already, but I have some other things I'm working on atm (that, and things like classes, and other real life things X) )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much lovely worldbuilding, in a world that's already been destroyed, no less. You might consider writing a few tales about Fantasy before the war. They might be a bit too tangential to fit here, but they'd be perfect for the Library.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uhhh

The one that times its movements to eye saccades? It's like a lizard or something? c.c I forget.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, 1516 was fun to read. Glad you liked the Notepeople! They were actually the second idea I came up with after the Bumpkle, after I had doodled humans made of music notes. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say no, since it seems a little strange to have the same piece in two places. I don't think there are any repeats between the sites; we do have some dual works that link to each other though (one piece on scp wiki, one piece on wanderers' library, both refer to each other), and those are cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome! Glad you liked it! ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was an amazing read. Thank You for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if You are allowed to post it there as well as here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, which SCP did you think the Uünsen was tied to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I spent the whole time wondering what SCP(s) this might be related to. The Uünsen seemed to be about the only one; I'd forgotten about 1762, but that gives me better appreciation for both the article and this tale. Anyway, not all of these entries are equally great, but I particularly like the Door Shards, the Titan, the Sun Stealers and the Monochrome Isle saga.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I particularly enjoyed the Notepeople, who bear some aspects with SCP-1516.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your writing would definitely fit in home over there <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic! Yes, I believe I saw this when it was still a draft. Well done +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does have a fairy tale vibe that fits with the Library, but it's related to SCP-1762, as an insight to the Fantasy land lost when the dragon box was destroyed. So that's why it's here. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but I feel it'd be more appropriate over at the Wanderers' Library.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It most certainly has been an awesome year and this was the perfect story to end it with. I can't wait to see what works you'll make in the upcoming year!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a really neat thing to help write. Thank you Goomy, for coming up with this and letting me collab!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this project took a long time. It's so exciting to see it finally posted.

So, first off, thank yous.

Thank you to anyone who reviewed this; you all are awesome in making this piece come together.

An especially big thank you to DreamwalkerFae, the person who reached out and suggested a collaboration on this project. Together, we completed twenty-six entries on some pretty amazing and imaginative creatures if I say so myself.

I hope you enjoy reading this. Welcome to the realm of Fantasy, a world where dragons fly, and magic whispers in every part of the forest, sea, mountain, and sky.

EDIT: Also, seeing as this is probably the last thing I'm writing before 2016, have a Happy New Year! It's been fun!
